name: Horn of Africa
location: Northeast Africa (Somalia, Ethiopia, Eritrea, Djibouti, Kenya)
conservationEfforts: Limited formal conservation due to political instability. Some community-managed rangelands in Ethiopia and Kenya. Somali wild ass conservation efforts in Eritrea. Djibouti established Day Forest National Park for endemic species.
description:
The Horn of Africa hotspot covers the arid and semi-arid lands of Somalia, Djibouti, Eritrea, Ethiopia, and northeastern Kenya. Despite its arid appearance, this region harbors surprising biological richness and exceptionally high plant endemism, with 55% of plant species found nowhere else.

The hotspot's biodiversity is shaped by its position at the junction of three biogeographic regions: Afrotropical, Palearctic, and Oriental. The landscape includes coastal deserts, dry savannas, juniper forests, and montane habitats. It is one of the most understudied hotspots due to ongoing political conflicts.
